rewritten_text: Grand opening marketing is justified only after model is proven and location can sustain full capacity on word-of-mouth alone within 6 months. Playbook: overstaff (25-30% excess capacity), allocate 10-15% of first-year revenue to ad spend across all channels, and run limited-time promotions (premium concepts: invite-only). This short-burst strategy converts high trial-to-customer conversion rates (40-60%) vs. ongoing marketing (8-12%).
notes: Hortative 'Never do grand opening until' (A8), loose axis 'short burst' (A3). Rewrite adds numbers + mechanism.

rewritten_text: Customer acquisition arbitrage: negotiate vendor wholesale price (e.g., $50 per training session), sell at market rate ($1,000), and retain spread ($950) as pure margin. Example (local gym): trainer cost is fixed whether delivering to 3 or 6 clients in semi-private format, so incremental customer cost is minimal. Method: negotiate 100-unit volume discounts before scaling acquisition spend.
